chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – Zakāt on the wealth of the orphans

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/76)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/76)
text: هُمَا قَالَا لَيْسَ عَلَى مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ فِي الدِّينِ وَ الْمَالِ الصَّامِتِ شَيْ‏ءٌ فَأَمَّا الْغَلَّاتُ فَعَلَيْهَا الصَّدَقَةُ وَاجِبَةٌ .

en.hubeali: Hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz, from Zurara and Muhammad Bin Muslim, both having said,
‘There is nothing upon the wealth of the orphans regarding the debts and the silent wealth (immovable asset). But, as for the produce, so upon it is the Obligatory charity (Zakāt)’.
en.sarwar: 5. Hammad ibn ‘Isa has narrated from Hariz from Zurarah and Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “Zakat is not due on loans people owe to orphans’ as well as on the orphans immoveable assets; however, it is obligatory to pay Zakat for the orphans’ grains.’”
